question: [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket/collateralParams for-loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition of supplying market.collateralParams with MAX_COLLATERALS (128) entries trigger touchMarket() to iterate all 128 CollateralParams in the for-loop at lines 762-773 of Midnight.sol, forcing every subsequent take/withdraw/repay/supplyCollateral/withdrawCollateral/liquidate caller to ABI-decode and pass the full 128-entry CollateralParams array in calldata on every call, violating the invariant that market creation parameters must not impose unbounded calldata gas costs on unrelated users, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS via calldata inflation on all market entry points? Proof idea: fuzz market.collateralParams.length from 1 to 128 and measure calldata gas of take() and liquidate() as a function of collateral count; assert gas scales linearly and document the maximum.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy/collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition of activating MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) collaterals in a market trigger isHealthy() to execute 16 consecutive IOracle.price() external calls inside the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop, causing take() (line 476) and withdrawCollateral() (line 568) to consume gas proportional to 16 * oracle_gas, violating the invariant that health checks must not be blockable by collateral count, causing scoped impact: fund freeze via gas exhaustion for any seller with 16 activated collaterals? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracle stubs each consuming near-maximum gas, activate all 16 collaterals, call take() as seller and withdrawCollateral() and assert both succeed within block gas limit.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate/collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition of a borrower having 16 activated collaterals trigger the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop in liquidate() (lines 607-618) to call IOracle.price() 16 times and accumulate maxDebt and badDebt across all iterations, causing liquidate() to consume gas proportional to 16 * oracle_gas even when only one collateral is being seized, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable regardless of collateral count, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure via gas exhaustion when borrower has maximum activated collaterals? Proof idea: fuzz collateral count from 1 to 16 with expensive oracle stubs and assert liquidate() always succeeds within block gas limit.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall/unbounded calls array] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition of constructing a calls[] array with no enforced length limit trigger multicall() (lines 211-220) to delegatecall an arbitrary number of take() operations in a single transaction, causing block gas exhaustion and memory expansion costs proportional to calls.length, violating the invariant that multicall must not bypass per-action gas invariants, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS blocking other users from submitting transactions in the same block? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to 1000 with minimal take() calls and measure gas consumption; assert no single multicall can consume more than a safe fraction of block gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: priceToTick/binary search while-loop] Can an unprivileged periphery caller under the precondition of passing price=1 (minimum valid price above zero) and spacing=1 trigger priceToTick() (lines 56-68) to execute ceil(log2(MAX_TICK)) = ceil(log2(5820)) = 13 iterations of the while(low != high) binary search, each calling tickToPrice() which calls wExp() with unchecked arithmetic, causing excessive gas consumption in any on-chain contract composing priceToTick() with routing logic, violating the invariant that tick conversion must be gas-bounded and predictable, causing scoped impact: gas griefing in any periphery contract using priceToTick for on-chain price routing? Proof idea: unit test priceToTick(1, 1) and priceToTick(1e18-1, 1) and measure exact iteration count and gas; assert bounded by 14 iterations.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: priceToTick/spacing rounding past MAX_TICK] Can an unprivileged periphery caller under the precondition of passing a spacing value that does not divide MAX_TICK (5820) trigger priceToTick() to return a tick value greater than MAX_TICK via the rounding expression (low + spacing - 1) / spacing * spacing at line 67, causing any subsequent tickToPrice() call with that result to revert with TickOutOfRange(), violating the invariant that priceToTick output must always be a valid input to tickToPrice(), causing scoped impact: fund freeze if priceToTick result is used as offer.tick in take()? Proof idea: fuzz spacing values that do not divide 5820 (e.g., spacing=7, 11, 13) and assert priceToTick(price, spacing) <= MAX_TICK for all valid price inputs.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket/collateralParams for-loop + IdLib.storeInCode CREATE2] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition of creating a market with 128 collaterals trigger touchMarket() to call IdLib.storeInCode() which deploys a CREATE2 contract storing the full abi.encode(market) payload (128 CollateralParams * ~128 bytes each = ~16KB), causing the first touchMarket() call to consume gas proportional to the CREATE2 deployment of a ~16KB contract, violating the invariant that market creation gas must be bounded and predictable, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where 128-collateral market creation approaches block gas limits? Proof idea: measure CREATE2 deployment gas in IdLib.storeInCode() for Market structs with 1, 32, 64, 128 collaterals and assert linear scaling with a safe upper bound.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + take with 128-collateral market + isHealthy] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition of composing a multicall with N take() calls each referencing a 128-collateral market where the seller has 16 activated collaterals trigger O(N * 128) calldata decoding and O(N * 16) oracle calls via isHealthy(), causing the multicall to consume gas proportional to N * (128 * calldata_cost + 16 * oracle_gas), violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify per-call gas costs unboundedly, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS via multicall gas amplification blocking batch trading? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 50 with 128-collateral markets and 16-collateral sellers; assert total gas stays within block limit.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy/collateralBitmap while-loop + take seller gas griefing] Can an unprivileged sell-offer maker under the precondition of activating 16 collaterals with gas-heavy oracle contracts trigger isHealthy() at line 476 of take() to execute 16 oracle calls that the taker must pay for in gas, causing each take() against the maker's sell offer to cost 16 * oracle_gas more than expected, violating the invariant that taker gas cost must not be controlled by the maker's collateral configuration, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a maker forces all takers to pay for expensive health checks? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles each consuming 50k gas, measure take() gas for sell offers where seller has 1 vs 16 activated collaterals.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan/unbounded tokens loop] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition of passing a tokens[] array with no enforced length limit trigger flashLoan() (lines 737-752) to execute two separate for-loops over tokens.length iterations (one safeTransfer, one safeTransferFrom), causing gas consumption proportional to 2 * tokens.length * transfer_gas, violating the invariant that flashLoan must not be a vector for unbounded gas consumption, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS blocking other transactions in the same block? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 500 and measure gas; assert a safe upper bound exists and document the maximum feasible tokens.length.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate/collateralBitmap while-loop + bad debt realization] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition of a borrower having 16 activated collaterals with bad debt trigger the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop to compute both maxDebt and badDebt across 16 oracle calls, then execute bad debt socialization math (lossFactor update, continuousFeeCredit scaling), causing liquidate() to consume gas proportional to 16 * oracle_gas + bad_debt_math, violating the invariant that bad debt realization must not be blockable by collateral count, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure and fund freeze when bad debt cannot be realized due to gas limits? Proof idea: create a 16-collateral undercollateralized position and measure liquidate() gas with expensive oracle stubs; assert it fits within block gas limit.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket/collateralParams for-loop + multicall market creation] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition of creating M distinct 128-collateral markets trigger a multicall that calls touchMarket() for each market, causing O(M * 128) collateral validation iterations and O(M) CREATE2 deployments in a single transaction, violating the invariant that market creation gas must be bounded per transaction, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS via multicall-amplified market creation gas exhausting block gas? Proof idea: fuzz M from 1 to 20 with 128-collateral markets in a single multicall and measure total gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy/collateralBitmap while-loop + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition of having 16 activated collaterals trigger isHealthy() inside withdrawCollateral() (line 568) to execute 16 oracle calls, causing withdrawCollateral() to revert or consume excessive gas when oracles are expensive, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must remain available to healthy borrowers, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where a healthy borrower cannot withdraw collateral due to gas exhaustion from 16 oracle calls? Proof idea: activate 16 collaterals with expensive oracle stubs, call withdrawCollateral() and assert it succeeds within gas limits.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: wExp/unchecked left shift + tickToPrice at tick=0] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition of crafting an offer at tick=0 trigger wExp() with the maximum positive argument LN_ONE_PLUS_DELTA * int256(MAX_TICK/2) = 0.004987541511039073e18 * 2910, causing the unchecked left shift uint256(expR) << uint256(q) at line 39 to overflow when q is large, producing an incorrect price from tickToPrice(0), violating the invariant that tickToPrice must return a value in (0, 1e18] for all valid ticks, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption in take() where buyerAssets/sellerAssets are computed from an overflowed price? Proof idea: unit test tickToPrice(0) and assert result <= 1e18 and > 0; verify wExp argument range for all ticks in [0, MAX_TICK].

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: priceToTick/binary search + divHalfDownUnchecked rounding] Can an unprivileged periphery caller under the precondition of passing price values near PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP (1e12) boundaries trigger priceToTick() to return a tick T where tickToPrice(T) is the nearest multiple of PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP >= price, but the rounding expression (low + spacing - 1) / spacing * spacing at line 67 rounds up to T+spacing when low is not a multiple of spacing, violating the invariant that priceToTick must return the lowest spacing-multiple tick with price >= input, causing scoped impact: offer placed at a higher tick than necessary giving the maker a worse price? Proof idea: fuzz price in [0, 1e18] and assert tickToPrice(priceToTick(price, spacing)) >= price and tickToPrice(priceToTick(price, spacing) - spacing) < price for all valid spacing values.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall/delegatecall + memory expansion] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition of composing a multicall with N calls each passing a 128-collateral Market struct trigger quadratic memory expansion costs in the delegatecall loop at lines 213-219, causing the multicall to consume gas proportional to N^2 due to EVM memory expansion pricing when each call allocates a large memory buffer for the Market struct, violating the invariant that multicall gas must scale linearly with call count, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a small number of memory-heavy calls exhausts block gas? Proof idea: measure multicall gas for N calls with 128-collateral Market structs and assert gas scales linearly not quadratically.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate/collateralBitmap while-loop + maxDebt accumulation overflow] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition of a borrower having 16 activated collaterals each with collateral amount near type(uint128).max and oracle price near ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE trigger the while loop to accumulate maxDebt via repeated mulDivDown additions at line 613, causing maxDebt to overflow uint256 and wrap to a small value, causing the health check originalDebt > maxDebt to incorrectly pass for a healthy position, violating the invariant that healthy positions cannot be liquidated, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure of collateral from a healthy borrower? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts and oracle prices near type(uint128).max and assert maxDebt accumulation does not overflow uint256.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket/collateralParams for-loop + repay calldata cost] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition of repaying debt in a 128-collateral market trigger touchMarket() inside repay() to require passing the full 128-entry CollateralParams array in calldata, causing repay() to cost O(128 * sizeof(CollateralParams)) in calldata gas regardless of the repayment amount, violating the invariant that debt repayment must not be prohibitively expensive due to market configuration, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where repayment becomes economically infeasible due to calldata cost in 128-collateral markets? Proof idea: measure repay() calldata gas for markets with 1, 32, 64, 128 collaterals and assert the difference is bounded and economically feasible.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate/collateralBitmap while-loop + post-maturity mode oracle calls] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition of a post-maturity market with a borrower having 16 activated collaterals trigger the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop in liquidate() to call 16 oracles even though post-maturity liquidation only requires block.timestamp > market.maturity (not a health check), causing unnecessary gas consumption and potential revert if any oracle reverts, violating the invariant that post-maturity liquidation must not be blockable by oracle failures, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure post-maturity when any of 16 oracles reverts? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles where one reverts, call liquidate() with postMaturityMode=true and assert behavior; check if the bitmap loop runs before or after the liquidatability check.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + liquidate with 16-collateral bitmap] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition of composing a multicall with N liquidate() calls each targeting a different borrower with 16 activated collaterals trigger O(N * 16) oracle calls in a single transaction, causing the multicall to exhaust block gas, violating the invariant that multicall liquidation must not be blockable by collateral count amplification, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS preventing batch liquidations of multiple borrowers? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 20 liquidate() calls in a multicall with 16-collateral borrowers and measure total gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: priceToTick/binary search + tickToPrice monotonicity at PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP] Can an unprivileged periphery caller under the precondition of passing a price that is an exact output of tickToPrice() for some tick T trigger priceToTick() to converge to low=T in the binary search, then the rounding expression (low + spacing - 1) / spacing * spacing to return T when T is already a multiple of spacing, but return T+spacing when T is not a multiple of spacing, violating the invariant that priceToTick(tickToPrice(T), spacing) must return T when T is a multiple of spacing, causing scoped impact: offer placed at a higher tick than the maker's intended price? Proof idea: for all ticks T that are multiples of spacing, assert priceToTick(tickToPrice(T), spacing) == T.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall/delegatecall + transient storage LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition of composing a multicall with two take() calls where the first take sets the LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT for seller X via UtilsLib.tExchange() and the second take also targets seller X trigger the second take() to see wasLocked=true at line 444, causing it to skip the UtilsLib.tExchange unlock at line 475, leaving the transient lock set for the remainder of the transaction, violating the invariant that liquidation lock must be cleared after each take(), causing scoped impact: fund freeze where seller X cannot be liquidated in any subsequent call within the same transaction? Proof idea: compose multicall([take(seller=X), take(seller=X)]) and assert liquidationLocked(id, X) is false after the multicall completes.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket/collateralParams for-loop + supplyCollateral calldata] Can an unprivileged user under the precondition of calling supplyCollateral() in a 128-collateral market trigger touchMarket() to require passing the full 128-entry CollateralParams array in calldata on every call, causing supplyCollateral() to cost O(128 * sizeof(CollateralParams)) in calldata gas regardless of which single collateral is being supplied, violating the invariant that collateral supply gas must be proportional to the operation not the market size, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where supplying collateral in large markets is economically infeasible? Proof idea: measure supplyCollateral() calldata gas for markets with 1 vs 128 collaterals and assert the ratio is bounded.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: wExp/unchecked arithmetic + negative branch division by zero] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition of crafting an offer at tick=MAX_TICK (5820) trigger wExp() with the maximum negative argument LN_ONE_PLUS_DELTA * (int256(MAX_TICK/2) - int256(MAX_TICK)) = 0.004987541511039073e18 * (-2910), causing the unchecked division 1e36 / wExp(-x) at line 26 to produce a result near zero after divHalfDownUnchecked rounding, causing tickToPrice(MAX_TICK) to return 0 and sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee to underflow in take(), violating the invariant that tickToPrice must return a positive price for all valid ticks, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption or revert in take() for offers at MAX_TICK? Proof idea: unit test tickToPrice(MAX_TICK) and assert result > 0 and > any valid settlementFee.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + withdrawCollateral repeated calls] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition of composing a multicall with N withdrawCollateral() calls for a borrower with 16 activated collaterals trigger isHealthy() to be called N times in a single transaction, each executing 16 oracle calls, causing O(N * 16) oracle calls total, violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify health check costs unboundedly, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a single multicall exhausts block gas via repeated health checks? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 50 withdrawCollateral calls in a multicall with 16-collateral borrower and measure total gas.
